description: Implements ADR-2605190900 in 5 independently-shippable phases ordered to maximize operator value per phase. Phase 1 (heartbeat contract) is pure additive — costs nothing if no consumer reads beats yet. Phase 2 (dead-letter + bounded retry) is the single biggest user-visible jump: invisible 60s churn becomes a P1 inbox event. Phases 3-5 close the class of bugs that produced the 2026-05-19 postmortem.
feature: Runtime supervision layer — heartbeat, bounded retry, dead-letter, pool supervisor, endpoint discovery, liveness self-test

phases.name: Heartbeat contract — IHeartbeatPort + STDB worker_process table

tasks.done_condition: trait IHeartbeatPort { async fn beat(&self, role: &str, status: HeartbeatStatus, evidence: Option<String>) -> Result<()>; async fn list_alive(&self, role: &str, ttl: Duration) -> Result<Vec<HeartbeatRow>>; }. HeartbeatStatus enum: Healthy | Degraded | Stopping. Pure trait, no I/O.
tasks.name: Define IHeartbeatPort trait + Heartbeat domain type

tasks.done_condition: Table worker_process { worker_id: String, pool_id: String, role: String, pid: u32, host: String, registered_at: Timestamp, last_heartbeat_at: Timestamp, status: String }. Reducers: register_worker (insert), heartbeat (update last_heartbeat_at), deregister_worker (delete). Indexed by (pool_id, role).
tasks.name: STDB worker_process table + register_worker / heartbeat reducers
tasks.verify: spacetime publish + spacetime sql 'SELECT count(*) FROM worker_process' returns 0 on fresh deploy

tasks.done_condition: Calls register_worker on adapter construct, beat() invokes heartbeat reducer, Drop impl calls deregister_worker. Defaults: role read from HEX_AGENT_ROLE env, host from hostname crate, pid from std::process::id().
tasks.name: SpacetimeHeartbeatAdapter implementing IHeartbeatPort
tasks.verify: cargo test -p hex-nexus --test heartbeat_adapter_e2e — spawns adapter, asserts row appears in worker_process within 2s, drops adapter, asserts row removed within 2s

tasks.done_condition: Each long-running component creates a SpacetimeHeartbeatAdapter at startup and beats every 15s on a background tokio task. Role strings: 'sched-daemon', 'nexus-server', 'workplan-executor'. NOTE: sched.rs is on CRITICAL_FILES — this task may be Tier-C operator-only (autonomous agents can't write it). Adjust workplan execution accordingly.

matching_commits: 2588a4c574c039197ac1b5298f6aa5112dc9780f feat(p1.4): wp-idle-research-swarm — Unit test: idle-trigger fires after N=4 consecutive empty ticks, does NOT fire when last_sweep < 6h ago, DOES fire when last_sweep ≥ 6h ago.

tasks.name: Wire heartbeat into sched daemon + nexus + workplan_executor
tasks.verify: after restart, spacetime sql 'SELECT role, last_heartbeat_at FROM worker_process' shows ≥ 2 rows beating within 30s

phases.name: Bounded retry + dead-letter — replace silent re-enqueue loop

tasks.done_condition: Table dead_letter { task_id, kind, payload, last_error, attempt_count, first_failed_at, last_failed_at, original_priority }. Reducer dead_letter_record(...) appends; never updates. Reducer dead_letter_replay(task_id) moves the row back to sched_task pending and deletes the dead_letter row.
tasks.name: dead_letter STDB table + reducer
tasks.verify: spacetime sql 'SELECT count(*) FROM dead_letter' returns 0 on fresh deploy

tasks.done_condition: Reconciler tracks (task_id, attempt_count) in a per-process HashMap or via the brain-task's stored fields. On Nth failure (default N=5 over 10 min, configurable via HEX_DEAD_LETTER_THRESHOLD), call dead_letter_record + send P1 inbox notification. Stop re-enqueueing.
tasks.name: Dispatcher tracks attempt_count + escalates on N

tasks.done_condition: GET /api/dead-letter returns the full table. POST /api/dead-letter/<id>/replay invokes dead_letter_replay. Dashboard route #/dead-letter shows rows with task_id, kind, age, last_error, attempt_count, and a Replay button.
tasks.name: REST endpoint + dashboard surface
tasks.verify: curl /api/dead-letter returns JSON array; dashboard hashroute #/dead-letter renders; after P2.2's test fires, the seeded dead-letter row appears in the response

tasks.done_condition: Reconciler emits an improver_event { kind: 'retry_quota_exceeded', task_id } when dead-lettering. Improver discovers these as Hypotheses with source=DispatchRetryQuota so the loop can propose changes (e.g. raise N, or split the workplan).
tasks.name: Audit existing brain-task retries
tasks.verify: after a dead-letter event, hex sched improver discover --once returns a Hypothesis with source=DispatchRetryQuota

phases.name: Pool supervisor — reap zombies, ensure consumer-exists-before-publish

tasks.done_condition: Table worker_pool { pool_id, role, min_workers, max_workers, last_supervisor_tick_at }. trait IWorkerPoolPort { async fn ensure_consumer(role: &str) -> Result<ConsumerStatus>; }. ConsumerStatus: Alive | Degraded { age_secs } | None.
tasks.name: worker_pool table + invariant trait

tasks.done_condition: Reducer supervisor_tick(pool_id) runs every 15s. Walks worker_process WHERE pool_id = X. Removes rows whose last_heartbeat_at is older than 60s. Emits worker_pool_alarm event if live_count < min_workers (operator notification path lives in nexus, not the reducer).
tasks.name: Supervisor reducer: reap + spawn
tasks.verify: spacetime call supervisor_tick on a pool with a stale row deletes the row + appends a worker_pool_alarm row

tasks.done_condition: Background tokio task on nexus: every 60s walks /proc looking for [hex-agent] <defunct> entries owned by this user. For each, sends SIGCHLD to the ppid or logs ppid=1 cases that need manual cleanup. Records each to worker_pool_alarm.
tasks.name: Local process reaper for hex-agent zombies
tasks.verify: cargo test -p hex-nexus --lib orchestration::pool_supervisor::tests::detects_synthetic_defunct (uses unshare or just-finished child with delayed wait)

tasks.done_condition: Before dispatching a brain-task, call IWorkerPoolPort::ensure_consumer(role). On ConsumerStatus::None: skip dispatch, record a brain_task_blocked event, fire P2 inbox at most once per 5-min window. Today the dispatcher fires into the void; after this it fails closed.
tasks.name: Dispatcher: refuse to publish without consumer

tasks.done_condition: fn discover_stdb_endpoint() walks: 1) .hex/project.json coordination.host, 2) HEX_STDB_HOST env, 3) localhost:3033, 4) HEX_STDB_FALLBACK_HOST env. Returns first that responds to a /v1/ping within 2s. .hex/state.json is read-only telemetry; never an input.
tasks.name: Discovery hierarchy + on-failure re-validation

tasks.done_condition: Every STDB call wraps in a retry-with-rediscovery: on transport error, call discover_stdb_endpoint(), retry once. Log the host swap as an improver_event { kind: stdb_endpoint_drift, old, new }.
tasks.name: Re-discovery on connection failure
tasks.verify: integration test: start with HEX_STDB_HOST=127.0.0.1:bogus, expect adapter to fall through to localhost:3033 + succeed; improver_event recorded

tasks.done_condition: config_sync.rs no longer treats state.json as a source of truth for endpoint config. State.json continues to receive write-only telemetry (current STDB host, current swarm count, etc.) for the dashboard, but configuration always re-reads project.json + env.
tasks.name: state.json read-only enforcement
tasks.verify: manual scenario: edit .hex/state.json to point at a bogus host, restart nexus, nexus connects to project.json's host instead of state.json's

phases.name: hex doctor liveness — end-to-end self-test + CI gate

tasks.done_condition: hex doctor liveness emits a sched_task { kind: 'ping', payload: <uuid> }. The dispatcher's ping handler is a no-op that writes improver_event { kind: 'pong', related: <uuid> } within its handler. The CLI waits up to 60s for the pong, then reports per-stage timing (enqueued, claimed, executed, ponged).
tasks.name: Synthetic ping/pong flow
tasks.verify: hex doctor liveness exits 0 with a green stage table on a healthy system; exits non-zero with the first failing stage named on a broken one

tasks.done_condition: Dashboard top-bar badge reads from GET /api/liveness which returns the most recent doctor liveness result + age. Badge color: green if last result was Pass within 60s, yellow within 5min, red beyond. The current 'hex-nexus is running' badge upgrades to track end-to-end health, not process-alive.
tasks.name: Dashboard liveness badge
tasks.verify: dashboard renders the badge; killing the agent worker turns it red within 60s

tasks.done_condition: New workflow boots STDB + nexus + a hex-agent worker, runs hex doctor liveness, asserts exit 0. Required for branch protection. Today the only end-to-end gate is the integration step which is now a skip-friendly probe (PR #53b9b3fe). This is the actual liveness gate.
tasks.name: CI workflow runs doctor liveness on every push
tasks.verify: CI run on a push to a test branch where the dispatcher is broken fails the liveness workflow

tasks.done_condition: EVIDENCE.md gains a 'Liveness' section that lists the 2026-05-19 postmortem (the failure mode this workplan was authored to close) + the reproducer steps for hex doctor liveness exit 0 vs exit non-zero. Closes the loop the ADR opened.
tasks.name: Postmortem evidence appendix in EVIDENCE.md
tasks.verify: EVIDENCE.md contains the section + the reproducer commands work as documented
